params.name: The Birth of Venus
params.description:
On Mar 18, 1487 AD, the Italian painter Botticelli painted Birth of Venus for Lorenzo the Magnificent of the Medici family.
Birth of Venus depicts the moment when Venus has emerged from the sea in a shell. She is attended by two winds who blow her toward the shore, while a nymph is poised to wrap a cloak, decorated with spring flowers, around Venus to cover her nudity, her eyes wide with wonder as she gazes out on the horizon.
